commits.message: Complete MVP5 with lessons learned
commits.body:
Five experiments, two that failed,
But in the end, the feature sailed.
Copy, cut, and paste now work—
No RefCell panics left to lurk.

The lessons learned are written down:
Ask before you code, don't drown
In assumptions left unchecked.
Build success? Runtime wrecked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Make Cut act as Copy in terminal
commits.body:
In browser land, Cut cuts with flair,
But terminals have no text to spare.
So Cut now copies, a gentle disguise—
The same result, no compromise.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Document Experiment 4 success, plan Experiment 5
commits.body:
Experiment 4 is done, the Cut appears,
The menu shows it now, no more tears.
But terminal Cut just sits there, mute—
Experiment 5 will make it copy, not stay on mute.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add Cut to Edit menu action list
commits.body:
One line was all it took to mend,
The oversight that caused the end
Of Experiment 3's brief career—
Now Cut appears, the menu's clear!

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Plan Experiment 4 to fix Cut menu
commits.body:
The root cause found, the mystery solved:
A list explicit, where menus evolved.
One line forgotten, the Cut left behind—
Experiment 4 shall fix this bind.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Experiment 3 failed: Cut menu missing
commits.body:
I added the variant, the handler too,
CommandDef was set, I thought I was through.
But the menu stayed empty, Cut nowhere in sight—
An explicit list I failed to write.

A costly oversight, time and money spent,
On code that compiled but never went
To the menu bar where it should reside.
Lesson learned: check where menu items hide.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Plan Experiment 3 for Cut (Cmd+X)
commits.body:
Three files to change, a simple scheme,
To cut in the browser, fulfill the dream.
KeyAssignment gets a brand new friend,
The menu and handler follow the trend.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Document copy+paste success in browser
commits.body:
The clipboard works! The test came through,
Google's search bar proved it true.
Cut and terminal still await their turn,
But victory's sweet—a lesson learned.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add Cmd+C/V copy/paste for CEF browser
commits.body:
The first attempt did crash and burn,
Key events through the loop took a wrong turn.
RefCell cried "already borrowed!"—
Our hopes felt hollowed.

But frame.copy() saved the day,
A synchronous call, the simpler way.
No message loop, no macOS callback chain,
Just clipboard bliss without the pain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Plan Experiment 2 for copy/paste
commits.body:
From ashes of the first attempt we rise,
With frame.copy()—a simpler, wiser guise.
No message loops, no re-entrant snare,
Just direct calls to clipboard with care.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Document Experiment 1 failure
commits.body:
The plan was sound, the code compiled clean,
But RefCell said: "Already borrowed, friend."
CEF's message loop, a trap unseen,
Brought re-entrancy—a crashing end.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add logging strategy for copy/paste debug
commits.body:
When bugs hide in the dark,
Logs light the way like a spark.
[CEF CLIPBOARD] they'll say,
Guiding us through the fray.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Document research for copy/paste fix
commits.body:
We traced the path from menu to match,
Found every state we need to catch.
Browser mode? Check. Key codes? Here.
The implementation plan is clear.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Begin Experiment 1 for copy/paste
commits.body:
The log awaits, the quest begins,
Approach One enters—may the best code win.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add implementation approaches for copy/paste
commits.body:
Three roads diverged in the code so deep,
Each promising copy/paste to keep.
Browser-aware menus? Intercept the key?
Or toggle shortcuts dynamically?
We've mapped the paths—now choose and leap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Fix line numbers in keybinding docs
commits.body:
The docs had lines all wrong, you see,
Pointing to code that couldn't be.
Six-sixty claimed, but six-forty-two found,
Now every reference is sound.
Cmd+C/V? Not handled yet—
A note reminds us not to forget.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Fix keybinding priority order in docs
commits.body:
The docs had it backwards, I confess—
Menu was first, but that's not the process.
performKeyEquivalent wins the race,
Menu key matching takes second place.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Document keybinding interception flow
commits.body:
From menu to CEF, seven levels deep,
Where keyboard events must crawl and creep.
Each layer inspects, transforms, or blocks—
A labyrinth of handlers, selectors, and locks.

We mapped the maze from top to floor,
So future devs won't wonder anymore.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add Cmd+R/Cmd+Shift+R for browser reload
commits.body:
The Shift key got lost in translation,
Swallowed by raw key event interpretation.
We moved the handlers up the chain,
Now hard reload works again—
A tale of key event prioritization.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add Cmd+[/] for browser back/forward navigation
commits.body:
The browser knew not where to go,
When Cmd and bracket pressed just so.
CEF in OSR mode, you see,
Ignores such shortcuts stubbornly.
Now we catch them, call the shots—
Back and forward, connect the dots!

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Fix double key actions in CEF browser
commits.body:
Tab, Enter, Space, Escape—
Each pressed once, but acted twice!
CEF heard KEYDOWN, then KEYUP,
And doubled every action's price.
Now we skip the second call,
One press means one event, that's all.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Build and bundle the termsurf CLI tool
commits.body:
The scripts forgot to pack,
A CLI in the back.
Now termsurf rides along,
Where it belonged all along—
No more "command not found" flak!

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Rename WezTerm to TermSurf in user-facing places
commits.body:
A name by any other byte,
Would render just as bright.
WezTerm shed its former guise,
TermSurf now greets your eyes—
In menus, configs, CLI alike,
The surf is up, take flight!

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Respect system dark mode in CEF browsers
commits.body:
At dawn the app awakes and asks the Mac,
"Is darkness what you seek, or light instead?"
NSAppearance whispers secrets back,
And websites dress in colors fitting said.

No more shall pages blind the midnight coder,
The system's will is done, the theme respects.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Fix CEF browser color rendering with sRGB
commits.body:
The colors came through gamma's gate twice,
A washed-out world, not looking nice.
"I'm already sRGB!" the pixels cried,
But the GPU converted them, misapplied.

Now texture views declare the truth at last,
The double-correction era has passed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Only render CEF browsers for visible panes
commits.body:
The browser lingered, ghostly, out of place,
Blocking new tabs with its persistent face.
Now we check which panes the user sees,
And hide the browsers on inactive keys.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Enable GPU-accelerated CEF rendering
commits.body:
The browser toiled in software's slow embrace,
Each pixel copied, frame by sluggish frame.
Now shared textures fly at GPU pace,
And on_accelerated_paint stakes its claim.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Set CEF focus on first paint for text cursor
commits.body:
We tried on creation, but the browser wasn't ready,
We tried accelerated paint, but software held steady.
Now on_paint sets focus with unfocus-refocus dance,
And the blinking cursor appears on first chance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Manage CEF browser focus for text cursor display
commits.body:
The cursor refused to blink, invisible, shy,
No focus was set, and we wondered why.
Now browse mode grants focus, control takes it away,
And the caret appears where your text wants to stay.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Skip KEYUP for navigation keys in CEF browser
commits.body:
The arrow keys moved twice with every press,
Both down and up caused cursor to progress.
Now KEYUP we ignore for navigation's sake,
And cursors move just once—no more mistake.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Forward keyboard and mouse to CEF in browse mode
commits.body:
The browser sat idle, ignoring each tap,
No clicks would register, keys fell through the gap.
Now mouse and keyboard flow into the frame,
And Ctrl+C alone escapes the browser's claim.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Show helpful hints in control mode
commits.body:
"Control Mode" was rather terse,
Now users see the keys to press.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Simplify CEF MVP4 spec, remove insert mode
commits.body:
Three modes were one too many,
Insert mode had to go.
Now Browse and Control are plenty—
A simpler path to flow.

No stacking, just one browser per pane,
Two-phase rendering keeps buffers sane.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Fix control bar text with two-phase rendering
commits.body:
A buffer mapped cannot be mapped again,
The GPU protests with a panic refrain.
So we split the work into two distinct parts:
Background first, then text when mapping departs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Display URL in control bar for Browse mode
commits.body:
Where once "Browse Mode" plainly stood,
The URL now shows as it should.
Control mode keeps its simple name,
But browsing shows from whence you came.
Phase one complete—the address shown,
Phase two will track where you have flown.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add vertical margins to control bar text
commits.body:
The text once cramped, no room to breathe,
Now half a cell both top beneath.
Two rows it stands, a taller bar,
The letters float like a distant star—
With breathing room on every side,
The control bar wears its height with pride.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add Browse/Control mode switching for CEF browser
commits.body:
Two modes now dance within the pane:
Browse for surfing, Control to reign.
Ctrl+C toggles the modal state,
Enter returns—no need to wait.
The bar declares which mode you're in,
So users know before they begin.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add half-cell margin to control bar text
commits.body:
The text hugged the edge too tight,
A crowded look, not quite right.
Half a cell of breathing room,
Now the letters have space to bloom.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Render "Control Bar" text in browser control panel
commits.body:
A bar once blank, a void of space,
Now letters march to fill the place.
With parse_status_text we shape the words,
And render_screen_line draws the nerds—
Er, glyphs! In monospace they stand,
The terminal font, as we had planned.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Tune settle delay to 30ms
commits.body:
Ten was too quick, a hundred too slow,
Thirty's the tempo where smooth resizes flow.
Not quite a blink, but enough time to wait—
The Goldilocks zone for a texture's fate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Defer CEF resize until drag stops
commits.body:
The browser bounced and jittered so,
Each frame a new size, to and fro.
"Why render mid-drag?" we thought at last,
"Just stretch the texture, hold it fast!"
Now pixels wait in patient grace,
Till mouse at rest finds its place.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add control panel background above CEF browser
commits.body:
A browser pane stood tall and proud,
Its head touching the topmost cloud.
But now a panel sits on high,
One cell of background—by and by,
The browser dips its viewport down,
Making room for its control crown.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add CEF MVP4 plan for control panel and modes
commits.body:
Three modes walk into a bar:
Control, Browse, Insert—each a star.
"Ctrl+C to escape!" they say,
"Enter to browse, i to play!"
The URL field awaits your keystroke from afar.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Update MVP3 docs to reflect settle approach
commits.body:
The state machine grew complex and tall,
With queues and edges, it had them all.
But simpler won the day, you see:
Just wait ten milliseconds, then render free.

Ctrl+Shift+R stands ready in the wings,
For when the texture misbehaves and clings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add settle-and-rerender after resize stops
commits.body:
Ten milliseconds, a patient wait,
For textures to align with fate.
While resize storms rage wild and fast,
We hold our brush until the last.

When silence falls and changes cease,
One final render brings us peace.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add Ctrl+Shift+R to force CEF browser re-render
commits.body:
When textures misbehave and pixels go astray,
A simple keystroke saves the day.
Ctrl+Shift+R, the magic spell,
Makes distorted browsers render well.

(Key-up, not down—macOS won't relay
The key-down event in Shift's array.)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Revert simple resize approach (bouncing)
commits.body:
The simple path we tried to take,
But bouncing browsers make hearts break.
We step back now to try anew,
With debouncing in our view.

This reverts commit 417c36e75.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add simple browser resize on pane change
commits.body:
We hoped that CEF would debounce inside,
But alas, the browser still bounces wide.
The simple approach did not suffice—
We'll need debouncing, to be precise.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Clarify resize call sites and pixel types in docs
commits.body:
Physical or logical? The question was unclear,
Now comments explain it, the path is now clear.
From paint or on_paint? Both places we call,
The docs now describe it—no ambiguity at all.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Update technical approach to match implementation
commits.body:
The old approach spoke of grids and such,
But reality differed quite much.
Now the docs align
With the code so fine—
One source of truth we trust and clutch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles
